---
title: "Task Definition"
---

## Problem Statement

- **Domain**: [Research domain — e.g., tabular classification, sequence modeling, graph learning]
- **Input**: describe modality, shape, and expected distribution
- **Output**: describe target space (class labels, continuous values, structured objects)
- **Why it is hard**: state the core technical difficulty in ≤ 2 bullets

```{dot}
//| label: fig-problem
//| fig-cap: "Problem framing: from domain to evaluation"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.2
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.55]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.6, height=0.42, margin="0.15,0.08"]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    Domain [label="Domain", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    Input  [label="Input Data", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    Output [label="Target Output", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Constraint [label="Constraints", fillcolor="#343a40"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    M1 [label="Metric A"]
    M2 [label="Metric B"]
    M3 [label="Metric C"]
  }

  Domain -> Constraint
  Input  -> Constraint
  Output -> Constraint
  Constraint -> M1
  Constraint -> M2
  Constraint -> M3
}
```

---

## Research Questions

- **RQ1**: [Primary RQ — the main question this work answers]
- **RQ2**: [Secondary RQ — e.g., what factors drive the result?]
- **RQ3**: [Tertiary RQ — e.g., does the method generalise?]

Each RQ should be falsifiable and tied to at least one metric in the evaluation protocol.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-rq
//| fig-cap: "Research questions mapped to evaluation protocol"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.2
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.5, height=0.42, margin="0.15,0.08"]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    RQ1 [label="RQ1", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    RQ2 [label="RQ2", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    RQ3 [label="RQ3", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    H1 [label="Hypothesis 1"]
    H2 [label="Hypothesis 2"]
    H3 [label="Hypothesis 3"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    E1 [label="Experiment 1", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    E2 [label="Experiment 2", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    E3 [label="Experiment 3", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  RQ1 -> H1 -> E1
  RQ2 -> H2 -> E2
  RQ3 -> H3 -> E3
}
```

---

## Scope and Constraints

- **In scope**: list what this work explicitly covers
- **Out of scope**: list what is intentionally excluded
- **Assumptions**: list simplifying assumptions (e.g., i.i.d. data, fixed compute budget)

```{dot}
//| label: fig-scope
//| fig-cap: "Scope boundary"
//| fig-width: 6
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.6]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    In [label="In Scope", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    Assume [label="Assumptions", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    Out [label="Out of Scope", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Budget [label="Compute Budget"]
    Data   [label="Data Protocol"]
    Metric [label="Eval Metric"]
  }

  In     -> Data
  Assume -> Budget
  Assume -> Data
  Out    -> Metric [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
  Data   -> Metric
  Budget -> Metric
}
```

---

## Evaluation Protocol

- **Primary metric**: [e.g., macro-F1, RMSE, mAP] — justify why this is the right metric
- **Secondary metrics**: [e.g., per-class accuracy, latency, memory] — diagnostic value
- **Baseline(s)**: the minimum bar to beat (e.g., linear model, published SOTA)
- **Dataset split**: train / val / test ratios; any k-fold cross-validation details
- **Statistical testing**: [e.g., paired t-test, Wilcoxon, bootstrap CIs] — report p-value threshold

```{dot}
//| label: fig-eval
//| fig-cap: "Evaluation chain: data → model → metric → decision"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.4
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.5, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  Data [label="Test Set", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  Model [label="Trained Model"]
  Primary [label="Primary\nMetric", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  Decision [label="Accept /\nReject", fillcolor="#4a2040"]

  Data -> Model -> Primary -> Decision
}
```
